data.title: NYC mayor-elect Mamdani calls for solidarity with striking Starbucks baristas
data.description:
New York City mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani is calling for New Yorkers to boycott Starbucks (SBUX) to send a “powerful message” to the Seattle coffee chain after some of its baristas walked off the job.

“While workers are on strike, I won’t be buying any Starbucks, and I’m asking you to join us,” Mamdani posted on X. “Together, we can send a powerful message: No contract, no coffee.”

On Thursday, 12,000 unionized Starbucks (SBUX) employees across the U.S. went on strike coinciding with Red Cup Day, the company’s annual holiday promotion. The Starbucks Workers Unite union initiated the strike to address “stonewalling” by Starbucks management pertaining to a new contract providing higher take-home pay, better hours, and address numerous claims of unfair labor practices.  

As the striking employees represent less than 4% of Starbucks’ (SBUX) workforce, the stoppage is unlikely to adversely affect the company’s operations.

However, NYC currently has the most Starbucks’ (SBUX) restaurants than any other U.S. city with roughly 200 locations across Manhattan. While it’s unlikely New York’s newly elected socialist mayor will mobilize all New Yorkers against the coffee chain, a protracted strike could amplify reputational pressure in one of its most visible markets.

Consequently, Mamdani’s post weighed on shares and drove SBUX to its session low of $84.55.

data.title: Saudi wealth fund PIF slashes Q3 stakes, keeps Uber, EA, few others
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund unloaded most of its stock holdings in Q3 2025, while holding on to Uber Technologies (UBER), Lucid Group (LCID), Electronic Arts (EA), Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) and Claritev (CTEV), according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Friday.

Among the higher-profile names the Public Investment Fund exited during the three-month period include Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Bank of America (BAC), Broadcom (AVGO), Coca-Cola (KO), Home Depot (HD), Eli Lilly (LLY), Nvidia (NVDA), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), UnitedHealth (UNH), Prologis (PLD) and Netflix (NFLX).

The firm did not make any new buys during the quarter, and the positions PIF kept were untouched, the 13F filing showed.

data.title: UBS spotlights 30 disrupters for the next decade
data.description:
UBS has unveiled a curated list of 30 companies it believes are best positioned to thrive over the coming decade, emphasizing that the next wave of market leadership will be driven by technology-enabled disruption across multiple industries.

In an investor note, the firm argues that the most significant opportunities through 2030 will emerge from greentech, fintech, and healthtech, along with the broader universe of enabling technologies such as artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. These areas, UBS noted, are poised to generate the innovations that redefine traditional business models and reshape competitive dynamics. 

According to the investment bank, companies leading these transformative segments are likely to outpace the broader market in earnings growth as long-term, structural tailwinds—ranging from digital adoption to sustainability-focused investment—continue to accelerate. 

Outlined below are the 30 names ranked by year-to-date performance: 

No. 1: Societe Generale (OTCPK:SCGLY) +138.3%

No. 2: Rolls-Royce Holdings (OTCPK:RYCEY) +105.5%

No. 3: Alibaba (BABA) +87.8%

data.title: Molina nabs Florida Medicaid contract
data.description:
• Molina Healthcare (MOH) was awarded a contract from the Florida Agency for Health Care Administration to provide coverage to about 120,000 enrollees in the Statewide Medicaid Managed Care and Children's Health Insurance Programs.
  • Enrollees are part of the state's Title XIX and Title XXI Children’s Medical Services Program for those with special healthcare needs. 
  • Molina noted that in 2025, total premiums for the program are expected to be ~$5B.

data.title: Iterum projects $5M–$15M in 2026 ORLYNVAH sales while advancing streamlined commercialization and payer access
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Iterum Therapeutics (ITRM) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Corey Fishman, President, CEO & Director, opened by emphasizing the early progress in commercializing ORLYNVAH following its August market launch and highlighted "sharing information about the early stages of the ORLYNVAH commercialization."
  • Christine Coyne, Chief Commercial Officer, detailed initial uptake, noting "through November 12, we generated more than 280 prescriptions which were driven by more than 100 unique prescribers" and that "nearly half of those prescribers have broadened their experience by prescribing ORLYNVAH to more than 1 patient."
  • Coyne reported on payer access and distribution, stating "we have a signed rebate agreement with one of the top 3 Medicare Part D pharmacy benefit managers," enabling future coverage, and noted "coverage now reaches 16% of insured lives with increasing adoption by employer groups and payer formularies."
  • Fishman announced an adjustment in sales strategy, sharing "we reduced the in-person field team to 10 representatives from our original plan of 20 and are in the process of augmenting our efforts with both in-person and virtual sales representatives."
  • Fishman also discussed financial planning, stating "we currently expect our full year 2026 net product revenue to be in a range between $5 million and $15 million," and noted, "our existing cash and cash equivalents provides an operating runway into the second quarter of 2026."
  • Judith Matthews, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "net product revenues were $0.4 million in the third quarter of 2025 with the launch of ORLYNVAH in the United States in August 2025..."

Outlook

  • Iterum guided for "full year 2026 net product revenue to be in a range between $5 million and $15 million."
  • Total operating expenses for 2026 are expected to range between $25 million and $30 million.
  • The company indicated, "cash and cash equivalents together with $2.6 million of net proceeds raised under our at-the-market offering program...will be sufficient to fund our operations into the second quarter of 2026."
  • Fishman explained, "in order to continue commercialization throughout 2026 and we will need to raise more capital."
  • No explicit comparison to analysts' estimates was possible due to lack of data in the prompt.

Financial Results

  • Net product revenues for Q3 2025 were $0.4 million, which "included some stocking at our specialty pharmacy."
  • Total operating expenses were $8.1 million in the third quarter 2025.
  • Cost of sales expense for the quarter was $0.02 million.
  • Amortization of intangible assets was $0.3 million.
  • Research and development costs were $1.3 million, a decrease from the previous year's same period.
  • Selling, general and administrative costs were $6.5 million for the quarter, primarily due to commercialization activities.
  • Net loss on a U.S. GAAP basis was $9 million for Q3 2025.
  • Non-GAAP net loss was $7.3 million for Q3 2025.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at the end of September were $11 million.

Q&A

  • Antonio Arce, WestPark Capital, Inc.: Asked if launch metrics such as prescriptions, prescribers, and rep coverage will continue to be reported. Fishman replied, "we do plan to report the kind of the prescriptions, the growth, the number of physicians, the general information so people have a sense of how the launch is going."
  • Arce also asked for more details on drivers of 2026 net sales guidance, particularly uptake and payer coverage. Fishman responded that physician feedback "has been very good" and emphasized progress in gaining coverage, stating "we're approaching that 6-month time frame in kind of February time frame for us."
  • Jason McCarthy, Maxim Group: Queried about timing for formulary submissions and digital marketing strategies. Fishman confirmed, "we have now submitted our bids to all the 3 big PBMs as well as the big Medicare Part D plans... these will hopefully be coming in over the end of this quarter and the first quarter."
  • McCarthy asked about future disclosure of patient types and high-risk aspects, to which Fishman explained data would be "more anecdotal information from those prescribing physicians" due to privacy constraints.
  • Arce further inquired about augmenting rep coverage with virtual efforts. Fishman stated, "we will probably do a combination of both in field and virtual to get to that at least 28 effective territories, if not more."
  • On alternative marketing channels, Fishman said, "things like social media tend to be more effective nowadays than the traditional kind of journal ads...but we will continue to look at them."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a generally positive but cautious tone, pressing for specifics on metrics, commercialization drivers, and marketing strategy. Questions repeatedly referenced progress and the need for continued reporting of launch data.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, with Fishman frequently expressing optimism: "Feedback from physicians has been very good," and "we believe that those virtual reps can be a very important part of our organization."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's current tone was more operational and focused on early execution and adapting the commercial strategy, whereas the prior quarter's remarks were more anticipatory about the upcoming launch. Analysts' engagement increased this quarter, contrasting with no analyst questions previously.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter focused on tangible early launch results for ORLYNVAH, with 280 prescriptions reported and a shift to a blended in-person and virtual sales force model; the previous quarter centered on pre-launch planning, hiring, and supply chain readiness.
  • Revenue guidance for 2026 was introduced this quarter, whereas the last quarter highlighted market opportunity and readiness for launch without specific projections.
  • There was increased discussion of payer access progress and formulary agreements, compared to last quarter's focus on initial pricing and securing product supply.
  • Analyst participation and scrutiny increased, with detailed inquiries into operational execution and commercialization pace.
  • Management's confidence remained steady, with a shift from forward-looking excitement to practical discussions on execution and sales force efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Risks identified include the need for additional capital to fund operations beyond Q2 2026, the challenge of expanding payer coverage, and the reliance on a modest commercial infrastructure.
  • Management acknowledged "we will need to raise more capital" and are seeking shareholder approval to authorize additional share issuance.
  • The company highlighted the risk that "actual results may differ materially from those indicated by these forward-looking statements as a result of various factors outside our control."
  • The ongoing need to secure more payer coverage and the gradual nature of commercial uptake were openly discussed.

Final Takeaway

Iterum Therapeutics emphasized its initial commercial progress for ORLYNVAH, highlighting steady prescription growth, expanding physician engagement, and early advances in payer coverage. The company outlined a revenue target of $5 million to $15 million for 2026 while actively adapting its sales strategy to blend in-person and virtual efforts for broader reach and efficiency. Management signaled confidence in the product’s positioning and market potential, while also acknowledging the necessity of raising additional capital to sustain operations and fuel further growth in the coming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nasdaq turns selloff around amid valuation worries, big tech recovers
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) recuperating and turning green after investors sold off over worries about lofty valuations. Most "Mag 7" stocks are trading up.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.7%.

“We expect markets to grind higher – though likely with continued volatility. Big tech stocks continue to lead this market, and for good reason, since many of these companies are producing ample cash flows to fund this massive spending on AI,” said Carol Schleif, chief market strategist at BMO Private Wealth.

“While some market participants are worried about the increasingly debt financed nature of the spending, we remind investors that balance sheets across the hyperscalers are largely under-levered, with some room to increase their funding sources, which may include equity or even cash,” Schleif added.

Most of the seven “Magnificent” stocks were initially in the red, but now all -- but Alphabet (GOOG) -0.7% and Meta (META) -0.3% -- have reversed course and are in green territory.

Financials (XLK) is one of the sectors lagging the most, with big bank names such as Goldman Sachs (GS), American Express (AXP), Visa (V), and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) among the top decliners within the Dow ((DJI)).

“What is catching my eye is that while sentiment is leaning bullish among retail investors and Wall Streeters, corporate insiders are selling at the highest level since 2000. They have all kinds of reasons to sell shares, including for compensation, so there is usually little signal there. That may well be the case today but given the conversation about valuation and bubbles and lofty earnings goals, the current 25:1 sell/buy ratio is something that gives me pause,” said Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity.

Odds of a December 25-basis-point interest rate cut fell to below 50% on Thursday and are currently are at 51.6%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Economic releases that had been delayed due to the U.S. government shutdown could start to trickle down next week, giving investors and the Fed a lot to ponder on before the December monetary policy meeting.

On crypto, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped below $100,000 for the first time since May, and is currently at over $96,500.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.12%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.60%.

Wall Street's major indexes closed significantly lower on Thursday as heightened market sentiment due to the end of the U.S. government shutdown dissipated, and focus was turned to the economy.

More on markets:

data.title: Xpeng Q3 preview: Revenue set to rise 100% Y/Y, investors to focus on updates around humanoid, robotaxi
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle maker Xpeng (XPEV) is expected to post a 100% year-on-year jump in its third-quarter revenue. The company is scheduled to report earnings on November 14, before the opening bell.

Revenue is expected to come in around $2.87 billion in this quarter. The company is likely to register a loss per share of -$0.07 on the revenue, a nealy 70% improvement from the year-ago period. Over the last 2 years, XPEV has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Earlier this month, the company  launched its next-generation 'Iron' humanoid robot, sending the stock soaring. The company highlighted that IRON is on track for mass production, expecting large-scale humanoid manufacturing by 2026.

Analysts believe much of the market’s attention to be centred around the firm’s business plan and its product launches, including the robot and robotaxi.

Seeking Alpha analyst Ricardo Fernandez expects the firm’s volumes to increase 150% driven by the Sedan segment. Gross margins are expected to be flat, around 17.5%, and EBITDA losses should shrink to around -2%, with the potential to reach breakeven, the analyst said.

Fernandez additionally noted that unit volumes are likely to grow 124% in 2025 to 426k units, according to a consensus of 30 analysts’ estimates. This should allow XPeng to break even on cash costs/EBITDA.

He believes that, from this point forward, rising sales volumes should lift XPeng’s profitability, with consensus estimates projecting EBITDA margins to reach about 12% by 2030 or roughly $3.3k per vehicle.

“From 2026 onward, XPeng should see a consistent increase in margins from 0% in 2025 to 5% in 2027, as added volumes or every unit sold above the 450k level adds margin. This operating scenario should produce significant cash earnings (net income, depreciation, and stock-based compensation) growth of over 45% in the 2026-2028 period and GAAP Net Income to top US$1bn in 2028,” the analyst added.
